productTitle: MikroTik hAP ac lite US version
name: MikroTik hAP ac lite US version
title: MikroTik hAP ac lite US version
short_description: (USA) 650MHz CPU, 64MB RAM, 5x LAN ports, 802.11a/b/g/n/ac, 2.4GHz 2 chains + 5GHz 1 chain , RouterOS L4
description:
Enjoy a comprehensive and affordable home networking experience with the MikroTik hAP ac lite, a dual-concurrent 2.4/5GHz Access Point designed for exceptional 802.11ac performance. Featuring dual-chain 802.11b/g/n 2.4GHz wireless and single-chain 802.11a/n/ac 5GHz wireless capabilities, the hAP ac lite delivers seamless WiFi coverage across both frequencies, optimizing connectivity for various applications.

Compact yet robust, this device is equipped with a potent 650MHz CPU, effortlessly managing a spectrum of networking tasks. Supported by 64MB of RAM, it thrives in multitasking scenarios, ensuring smooth and efficient operations across diverse applications. The blend of a powerful CPU and ample RAM makes this device a versatile and reliable ally for all your networking needs.

At the heart of its operation is the RouterOS L4 license, the operating system developed by MikroTik for their routers and wireless systems. This license level, demonstrating a comprehensive feature set, empowers the hAP ac lite with advanced capabilities suitable for managing small to medium-sized networks.

The hAP ac lite is equipped with five 10/100Mbps Ethernet ports, each supporting data transfer rates of 10 or 100 megabits per second (Mbps). These ports serve as versatile connectors for various devices within your local network, ensuring seamless connectivity. Enhancing its capabilities, the hAP ac lite features PoE-Out on Port 5, allowing it to supply power to other PoE-compatible devices. This feature streamlines cabling and power distribution, making it ideal for powering devices like IP cameras. Additionally, the USB-A 2.0 port opens the door to external connectivity, specifically designed to support 3G or 4G modems.

The device further features Passive PoE Input, enabling power to be supplied either through the conventional power jack or seamlessly via a PoE injector. Passive Power over Ethernet (PoE) ensures a constant voltage delivery, enhancing the efficiency of power and data transmission through the same Ethernet cable.
